_meta.description: Reference biomechanical ranges for a +5 handicap / tour-level swing.
sources: Cheetham et al., Computer-aided method for analyzing golf swing (2001)
Hume, Keogh, Reid, Sports Med 2005: A review of full swing biomechanics

tempo_ratio.label: Backswing-to-downswing tempo ratio
tempo_ratio.fault_high: Downswing too rushed relative to backswing; loss of sequencing
tempo_ratio.fault_low: Backswing too quick or downswing too slow; loss of energy storage

x_factor.label: Shoulder-hip separation at top
x_factor.fault_low: Insufficient torso coil; reduced power potential

hip_open_impact.label: Hip rotation past square at impact
hip_open_impact.fault_low: Hips not clearing — leads to early extension and a blocked or hooked shot
hip_open_impact.fault_high: Hips spinning out — leads to loss of lag and weak slices

spine_stability.label: Spine angle variability across swing
spine_stability.fault_high: Posture changes during swing — inconsistent strike pattern

lead_arm_top.label: Lead arm angle at top of backswing
lead_arm_top.fault_low: Bent lead arm — collapses width and shortens swing arc

head_sway.label: Lateral head travel relative to shoulder width
head_sway.fault_high: Head sliding off the ball — inconsistent contact, fat/thin shots

weight_transfer.label: Weight on lead foot at impact
weight_transfer.fault_low: Hanging back on trail leg — adds loft, weak strike, fat contact

shaft_lean_impact.label: Forward shaft lean at impact
shaft_lean_impact.fault_low: Adding loft / scooping — high spin, weak ball flight
shaft_lean_impact.fault_high: Excessive lean — low launch, deloft errors
